# Transcrição: 01 Optional Faux Price

- **Fonte:** 01 Optional Faux Price
- **Duração:** 01:47
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:47]

We begin by introducing what's called a faux price. And this is a price that's roughly twice that of your actual price. It's a fake price. And this is done to anchor your viewer, your prospect, to a higher price before adding in all your bonuses. Notice that there's no bonuses that have been added into the grand offer yet. Okay? So after you do that, your actual price will be so much more appealing when you get to the offer. So we're going to tell the viewers that the value of your product, the value of your product, is X. And that X is going to be about twice what your, what your sell price is. Okay? Here's how this is going to work. Give you some example slides. I simply say the price of the every other day diet system sold by itself is valued at $97. So I've actually sold it for $97. So I could literally say $97, but I don't. I say it's valued at $97 because that's the initial value of it because it comes with all this other stuff. Right? So I anchored $97 in the in the prospects mind. Now at this point, you are probably thinking what I was thinking when I first discovered this. And this is one of those techniques. There's there's a few techniques that I discovered by looking at everybody else's winning video sales letters. This is one of them. My immediate reaction when I first saw this was like, oh, wow, 97 bucks. That's too expensive. I'm going to window out, but that's not what happens. I think the reason why this works is perceived value because you've just built up your product or service to have an extraordinary value. So when you deliver a price, even if the price is high, like if you got a $3,000 product, you know, and then you built up the value, they could be thinking it's $6,000 or $10,000. So if you say it's valued at $6,000, then they might be, oh boy, that's pretty expensive, but I'll keep watching.
